problem: Fix-plan queue lived only in SessionManager RAM. After MCP server restart, TTL expiry (30 min) or agent crash, selected_tasks / completed_task_ids / failed_task_ids were lost. Each new session re-ran drift_fix_plan and lost cross-session continuity, forcing ad-hoc micro-patches instead of coherent batch work.
change_summary: Introduced append-only JSONL event log at <repo>/.drift-cache/queue.jsonl.
Events: plan_created (snapshot), task_claimed/task_released (transient, dropped on replay), task_completed / task_failed (terminal, applied via replay).
drift_session_start replays the log by default; reduce_events picks the latest plan_created and applies all later terminal events.
New optional parameter fresh_start: bool = False on drift_session_start allows agents and tests to skip replay.
Thread-safe appends (threading.Lock + best-effort OS lock msvcrt/fcntl); corrupt lines are logged and skipped; rotation compacts the log at 10 MB by dropping transient events.

added_classes: TestRestartReplay (3 tests: resume, fresh_start opt-out, no log)

regression_check.command: pytest tests/test_mcp_catalog.py tests/test_mcp_copilot.py tests/test_mcp_orchestration_coverage.py tests/test_mcp_hardening.py tests/test_tool_metadata.py tests/test_issue_496_mcp_catalog_import_and_cache.py -q

policy_alignment.framing: fix (adoption-blocker for MCP fix-loop per Policy §8 Einführbarkeit)
policy_alignment.reasoning: Without cross-session queue continuity, agentic usage of drift cannot be empirically demonstrated — a direct blocker for the Distribution phase.

notes: STRIDE Tampering risk (local write access to .drift-cache/queue.jsonl) is mitigated because replayed tasks still pass through SG-005/SG-006/SG-007 strict guardrails before any fix-apply. Replay reconstructs plan + terminal outcomes only, not lease/metric state.
